connectorUiConfig.title: Forescout Host Property Monitor (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Forescout Host Property Monitor connector allows you to connect host/policy/compliance properties from Forescout platform with Microsoft Sentinel, to view, create custom incidents, and improve investigation. This gives you more insight into your organization network and improves your security operation capabilities.

sampleQueries.query:
union isfuzzy=true ForescoutHostProperties_CL, ForescoutPolicyStatus_CL, ForescoutComplianceStatus_CL
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 20

dataTypes.name: ForescoutHostProperties_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ForescoutHostProperties_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ForescoutPolicyStatus_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ForescoutPolicyStatus_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ForescoutComplianceStatus_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ForescoutComplianceStatus_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Microsoft.Insights/dataCollectionEndpoints
customs.description: Data Collection Endpoint is required for the modern push connector to receive data from the external security system.

customs.name: Microsoft.Insights/dataCollectionRules
customs.description: Data Collection Rule is required to define the schema and transformations for incoming data.

customs.name: Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments
customs.description: Role assignment is required to grant the Entra application permissions to send data to the Data Collection Rule.

customs.name: Forescout Plugin requirement
customs.description: Please make sure Forescout Microsoft Sentinel plugin is running on Forescout platform. Instructions on how to configure Forescout Microsoft Sentinel plugin are provided at Forescout Documentation Portal (https://docs.forescout.com/bundle/microsoft-sentinel-module-v2-0-0-h)

instructionSteps.title: 1. Create ARM Resources and Provide the Required Permissions
instructionSteps.description:
This connector receives data from external security systems that push logs to Microsoft Sentinel. The external system must be configured to send raw event data to the Microsoft Sentinel Ingestion API. Clicking on **Deploy** will trigger the creation of Log Analytics tables and a Data Collection Rule (DCR). It will then create an Entra application, link the DCR to it, and set the entered secret in the application. This setup enables data to be sent securely to the DCR using an Entra token.

[Learn more about the connector setup process](https://review.learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/create-push-codeless-connector)

parameters.content:
## Azure Resource Deployment

The deployment process will create the following resources:
- Log Analytics custom table(s)
- Data Collection Endpoint (DCE)
- Data Collection Rule (DCR)
- Entra ID Application Registration
- Role assignments for data ingestion

parameters.label: Deploy Forescout Host Property Monitor connector resources (Multi-Stream)
parameters.applicationDisplayName: Forescout Host Property Monitor (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)

parameters.content:
### Configuration Values

After deployment, copy the following values to configure your external security system:

### Stream Names (3 streams)

Use the appropriate stream name when configuring each data source:

parameters.text: Keep these values secure. You will need them to configure your external security system.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Configure your external system to push logs
instructionSteps.description:
Use the following parameters to configure your external security system to send logs to the workspace.

### Configuration Steps

1. Access your external security system's configuration interface.
2. Navigate to the data forwarding, integration, or SIEM settings.
3. Select **Azure Monitor** or **Microsoft Sentinel** as the destination.
4. Select **Data Collection Endpoint** as the authentication method.
5. Configure the required fields using the values from the previous step:
   - **Tenant ID**: Copy from above
   - **Application (Client) ID**: Copy from above
   - **Client Secret**: Copy from above
   - **Data Collection Endpoint**: Copy from above
   - **Data Collection Rule Immutable ID**: Copy from above
   - **Stream Name**: Copy the appropriate stream name from above
6. Save the configuration and enable/start the forwarder.

### Validation

> **Note**: Data will appear in the following tables in your Log Analytics workspace within a few minutes:
> - **ForescoutHostProperties**
> - **ForescoutPolicyStatus**
> - **ForescoutComplianceStatus**

To verify data is being received, run the following queries in your Log Analytics workspace:

**Table 1: ForescoutHostProperties**

```kusto
ForescoutHostProperties
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| take 10
```

**Table 2: ForescoutPolicyStatus**

```kusto
ForescoutPolicyStatus
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| take 10
```

**Table 3: ForescoutComplianceStatus**

```kusto
ForescoutComplianceStatus
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| take 10
```

**All Tables Combined:**

```kusto
union ForescoutHostProperties, ForescoutPolicyStatus, ForescoutComplianceStatus
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| take 10
```

### Troubleshooting

If data is not appearing:
- Verify the configuration values are correct
- Check that the forwarder/integration is enabled and running
- Review logs in your external security system for any errors
- Ensure network connectivity from your system to Azure
- Verify the Entra application has the correct permissions

parameters.content: After deploying the Azure resources, configure the Forescout Microsoft Sentinel plugin to send data to the deployed DCE/DCR endpoints for all three data types: **ForescoutHostProperties_CL**, **ForescoutPolicyStatus_CL**, and **ForescoutComplianceStatus_CL**.
For detailed configuration instructions, refer to the [Forescout Documentation Portal](https://docs.forescout.com/bundle/microsoft-sentinel-module-v2-0-0-h)

### Important: Connection Status

This is a **push-based connector** - it receives data from Forescout and does not maintain an active polling connection. The connector shows as "Connected" when data has been received within the last 30 days.

instructionSteps.title: Uninstall connector
instructionSteps.description: Disconnect from all sources and delete the connector instances.

parameters.content:
## 1. Disconnect from All Sources

Before deleting the connector, stop the external system from sending data to Microsoft Sentinel:

1. Access your external security system's configuration interface.
2. Navigate to the data forwarding, integration, or SIEM settings.
3. Disable or remove the Microsoft Sentinel / Azure Monitor integration.
4. Verify that data has stopped flowing by checking that no new rows appear in the table(s) in your Log Analytics workspace.

## 2. Delete the Connector Instances

Run the following PowerShell commands to remove the push connector instances:

```powershell
$sub = "<subscription-id>"
$rg  = "<resource-group-name>"
$ws  = "<workspace-name>"
$connector = "ForescoutHostPropertyMonitorPushConnector"

Write-Host "Deleting $connector..." -NoNewline
az rest --method DELETE --uri "/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/$ws/providers/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/dataConnectors/$connector`?api-version=2024-09-01"
Write-Host " Done" -ForegroundColor Green
```
> **NOTE**: Replace `<subscription-id>`, `<resource-group-name>`, and `<workspace-name>` with your actual Azure values before running the script.
This will remove the push connector instances. Historical data in your Log Analytics workspace will be retained.
